info.title: Email Gateway
info.description: Email gateway that routes emails to Postmark (transactional) or Instantly (broadcast) based on type.

tags.name: Email Routing
tags.description: Route emails to transactional or broadcast providers

success.description: Whether the email was sent successfully

messageId.description: Provider message ID (Postmark messageId or Instantly leadId)

deduplicated.description: True if this response was returned from the idempotency cache (email was not re-sent)

campaignId.description: Campaign ID for tracking and stats grouping

leadId.description: Lead ID for end-to-end tracking. When provided: logged on receipt, forwarded to the downstream provider (Postmark/Instantly), and returned as `messageId` in the response for broadcast sends. Omitting it means email stats cannot be correlated back to the lead.

workflowSlug.description: Workflow slug for tracking and grouping

idempotencyKey.description: Unique key to prevent duplicate sends. If a send with the same key already succeeded, the cached response is returned with `deduplicated: true` and no email is re-sent. Use a value unique per send attempt — e.g. the run ID, or a composite like `{runId}:{nodeId}` when a single workflow run triggers multiple sends.

from.description: Sender address, e.g. "Display Name <email@domain.com>". If omitted, the downstream provider resolves its own default.

inReplyTo.description: RFC 5322 In-Reply-To header — Message-ID of the email being replied to, including angle brackets

references.description: RFC 5322 References header — space-separated list of Message-IDs (each enclosed in angle brackets) representing the thread chain

messageStream.description: Postmark message stream ID. If omitted, postmark-service uses its configured default.

campaignId.description: Campaign ID for tracking and stats grouping

leadId.description: Lead ID for end-to-end tracking. When provided: logged on receipt, forwarded to the downstream provider (Postmark/Instantly), and returned as `messageId` in the response for broadcast sends. Omitting it means email stats cannot be correlated back to the lead.

workflowSlug.description: Workflow slug for tracking and grouping

idempotencyKey.description: Unique key to prevent duplicate sends. If a send with the same key already succeeded, the cached response is returned with `deduplicated: true` and no email is re-sent. Use a value unique per send attempt — e.g. the run ID, or a composite like `{runId}:{nodeId}` when a single workflow run triggers multiple sends.

subject.description: Shared email subject line (same thread, follow-ups are Re:)

sequence.description: Email sequence steps sent via Instantly

bodyHtml.description: HTML email body for this step

bodyText.description: Plain text email body for this step

daysSinceLastStep.description: Days to wait since the previous step (0 = immediate, step 1 is always 0)

contacted.description: Leads added to a campaign / send attempted

sent.description: Leads with at least 1 email sent (COUNT DISTINCT lead)

opened.description: Leads who opened at least 1 email

clicked.description: Leads who clicked at least 1 link

notSending.description: Leads in campaigns currently flagged with Instantly's not_sending_status diagnostic. Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

cancelled.description: Leads whose campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job (delivery_status='cancelled'). Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

opened.description: Unique emails opened at least once (COUNT DISTINCT)

clicked.description: Unique emails with at least 1 click (COUNT DISTINCT)

stepStats.description: Per-step breakdown (instantly broadcast only; empty/omitted for postmark transactional)

contacted.description: Whether at least one sending exists in this scope

sent.description: Whether an email was sent in this scope

delivered.description: Whether an email was delivered in this scope (sent AND not bounced)

opened.description: Whether the recipient opened any email in this scope

clicked.description: Whether the recipient clicked any link in this scope

replied.description: Whether the recipient replied in this scope (always false for postmark — no reply tracking)

replyClassification.description: Classification of most recent reply: positive / negative / neutral, or null if no reply (always null for postmark)

bounced.description: Whether an email bounced in this scope

unsubscribed.description: Whether the recipient unsubscribed in this scope

cancelled.description: Whether the campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job. Optional in contract v1; required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

lastDeliveredAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last delivery in this scope; null if none

brandId.description: Brand ID — if present without campaignId, activates brand mode: returns per-campaign breakdown (byCampaign) + aggregated brand status. If both brandId and campaignId are provided, campaign mode takes precedence and brandId is ignored. Omit both brandId and campaignId for org mode (global signals only).

campaignId.description: Campaign ID — if present, activates campaign mode: returns status scoped to this specific campaign. Takes precedence over brandId. Omit both brandId and campaignId for org mode (global signals only).

items.description: List of emails to check status for

post.description: Send a transactional or broadcast email via the appropriate provider.

get.description:
Returns email stats aggregated across providers.

**Without `groupBy`:** returns a flat `StatsResponse` with optional `transactional` and `broadcast` objects.

**With `groupBy`:** returns a `GroupedStatsResponse` — an object with a `groups` array. Each element has a `key` (the value of the groupBy dimension, e.g. a brand UUID when `groupBy=brandId`) and optional `transactional` / `broadcast` stats objects.

Use the `type` parameter to restrict to a single provider (transactional or broadcast).

200.description: Aggregated stats. Shape depends on whether `groupBy` is set — see `StatsResponse` (flat) vs `GroupedStatsResponse` (grouped).

get.summary: Get aggregated email stats (public, no identity headers required)
get.description:
Same behavior as `GET /orgs/stats` but does not require `x-org-id` or any identity headers. Intended for internal services (e.g. leaderboard) that don't have user context.

**Without `groupBy`:** returns a flat `StatsResponse`.

**With `groupBy`:** returns a `GroupedStatsResponse` — `{ groups: [{ key, transactional?, broadcast? }] }`. The `key` is the value of the groupBy dimension (e.g. a brand UUID when `groupBy=brandId`).

post.description:
Batch lookup of delivery status for a list of emails. `brandId` and `campaignId` are both optional — pass either, both, or neither.

**Three modes:**

| `brandId` | `campaignId` | Mode | Active fields |
|-----------|-------------|------|---------------|
| present | absent | Brand | `byCampaign` + `brand` + `global` |
| absent | present | Campaign | `campaign` + `global` |
| present | present | Campaign | `campaign` + `global` (brandId ignored) |
| absent | absent | Org | `global` only |

**Brand mode** (`brandId` without `campaignId`): returns a `byCampaign` object mapping each campaignId to its `StatusScope`, plus an aggregated `brand` scope (BOOL_OR across campaigns, `replyClassification` from most recent, `lastDeliveredAt` = MAX).

**Campaign mode** (`campaignId` present): returns a single `campaign` scope for that campaign.

**Org mode** (neither `brandId` nor `campaignId`): returns only the `global` block — org-wide bounce/unsubscribe signals. Useful for cross-brand, cross-campaign delivery checks (e.g. dashboards aggregating across all brands of an org).

Non-applicable fields are always present but set to `null`.

Returns status from both broadcast (Instantly) and transactional (Postmark) providers. If one provider fails, the other's results are still returned. If both fail, returns 502.

**Headers** (`x-brand-id`, `x-campaign-id`, etc.) are tracing/logging only — they are forwarded to downstream services but do NOT influence filtering logic. Filtering is driven exclusively by body fields.

brandMode.summary: Brand mode — per-campaign breakdown for a brand

orgMode.summary: Org mode — global signals only (no brandId, no campaignId)

200.description: Status results per email, split by provider (broadcast / transactional).

brandMode.summary: Brand mode response — byCampaign breakdown + aggregated brand

orgMode.summary: Org mode response — only global signals populated

400.description: Invalid request — empty items or invalid email

502.description: Upstream service error — both providers failed

post.summary: Forward Postmark webhook events to upstream postmark-service
post.description: Legacy passthrough. Forwards the body to the upstream postmark-service. Inbound (`RecordType=Inbound`) routing now happens via `POST /inbound/postmark` after postmark-service handles Postmark webhooks directly.

post.summary: Receive Postmark inbound payload from postmark-service and fan out to subscribers
post.description:
Internal service-to-service endpoint. Called by postmark-service when Postmark delivers an inbound email (`RecordType=Inbound`). The body is the raw Postmark inbound payload, untransformed.

**Fan-out:** matches the payload's recipients against `EMAIL_GATEWAY_SUBSCRIPTIONS` (env JSON). Every matching subscription receives a signed HTTP POST to its `endpoint_url`.

**Headers sent to each subscriber:**
- `content-type: application/json`
- `x-eg-signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hex sha256(t.body, secret)>` — HMAC-SHA256 over `${unix_seconds}.${body}` with the subscription's shared secret
- `idempotency-key: <Postmark MessageID>` — consumer must dedupe on this

**Fail loud:** if any subscriber returns non-2xx or the network call errors, this endpoint returns 502. The caller (postmark-service) propagates the 5xx to Postmark, whose 45-minute retry window then re-delivers the event.

**Auth:** `x-api-key` (shared service key, same one used across internal services).

200.description: Inbound payload dispatched to all matching subscribers (or no subscriber matched)

post.description: Receives Instantly webhook events and forwards them to the upstream instantly service
